title: sorbus aria var. umbellata
intro: Sorbus aria var. umbellata, also known as the Whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. Its bark, leaves, and fruits have been used in traditional medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, offering several health benefits, including anti-aging, antioxidant, and immune-boosting properties, as well as potential anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal preparations, Sorbus aria var. umbellata is often used in infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, and its extracts are also used in modern pharmaceutical applications, highlighting its potential as a valuable medicinal plant.
